aboutPage.title: Developer, product builder, and AI postgraduate
aboutPage.description: Connor has over {{yearsOfExperience}} years of development experience building production software across full stack, backend-first systems, mobile apps, and AI-led projects. He holds a first-class undergraduate degree from Kingston University and is currently studying for an MSc in Artificial Intelligence.

cards.yearsDescription: Professional and project-based development experience across multiple product types.
cards.educationDescription: Strong academic grounding from Kingston University, with advanced postgraduate AI study in progress.
cards.aiDescription: Experience applying machine learning and vision systems to solve concrete, real-world problems.

aboutPage.safeMapDescription: Safe Map is a safety-first routing application inspired by Connor's real-world experiences as someone living with cerebral palsy. The purpose of the app is to support people with disabilities and anyone who feels vulnerable walking alone. It helps people navigate through well-lit streets by using streetlight markers directly on the map so users can quickly identify better-lit paths, and includes a custom smart-routing algorithm that automatically adjusts recommended walking routes toward the most illuminated streets. It also uses tactile vibration cues to indicate left and right turns from inside a user's pocket, reducing the need to expose a phone while walking.
aboutPage.greenWasteDescription: A computer-vision project focused on identifying the most eco-friendly disposal pathway for bulky waste items, including recycling, repurposing, or incineration recommendations. The system uses modern CNN approaches, NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano hardware, and Intel RealSense depth cameras in a remote van-based setup so decisions can be made before collection.

contributions.one: Delivered technical work in healthcare contexts, including projects with the NHS.
contributions.two: Worked as an Academic Support Mentor at Kingston University for three years, helping students build confidence in software and technical concepts.
contributions.three: Comfortable across the full delivery lifecycle, from technical architecture and implementation to communication with users and stakeholders.

aboutPage.beyondDescription: He also performs professionally as a magician and mentalist. To keep this portfolio clear, that story has been split onto a dedicated page.

magicPage.title: Performance work beyond tech
magicPage.description: Connor has been performing for almost two decades, with a psychology and mentalism background spanning over {{yearsInPsychology}} years. His work combines close-up magic, psychological theatre, and audience connection for live events, charity audiences, and private bookings.

cards.yearsDescription: Nearly two decades of live performance experience across stage and close-up formats.
cards.internationalDescription: Performances delivered in Scotland, Wales, and the USA alongside events throughout the UK.
cards.audiencesDescription: Performances for community and charity events including NCS, plus private and industry audiences.

psychology.one: He has designed performance moments that use psychological techniques to gently steer people toward choosing what he wants them to, while still making it feel fair, natural, and fully like it was their own choice.
psychology.two: He has invented his own system that uses small behaviour clues, including emoji habits and the way people use them in messages, to make thoughtful observations about someone's character, personality and life in a performance setting.
psychology.three: He has created conversational game-based routines, including noughts and crosses (Tic-Tac-Toe), that help people feel comfortable, understood, and genuinely connected in a way that they might not have felt ever before.
psychology.four: He has successfully convinced people that he possesses supernatural abilities, including mind reading and telepathy, through the use of psychological techniques and performance skills. This has led to memorable moments of wonder and amazement for his audiences.

magicPage.notableDescription: He has performed for cast members of The Boys including Karl Urban, Laz Alonso, Jessie T Usher, Karen Fukuhara, and Shantel VanSanten, as well as Sebastian Stan, Freya Allan, Taylor Lautner, Daniel Panabaker, Jensen Ackles, and other public figures.
magicPage.consultingDescription: Alongside performing, he creates original magic effects and works as a consultant and advisor to other magicians for live shows, social media content, and television appearances.
magicPage.writingDescription: His performance approach is rooted in psychology and mentalism, and he has contributed to and written books that teach methods behind psychological influence, audience reading, and deep connection work.
magicPage.developerDescription: Looking for more detail on Connor's software engineering career and academic background? The dedicated About page gives an overview, and the CV page provides the full detail.

cv.title: Software Engineer
cv.summary: Computer Science graduate with hands-on experience in Python, Java, SQL, and Kotlin, with a strong interest in backend and mobile systems that connect to real-world APIs.
cv.profile: AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner with practical experience using Git-based workflows and collaborating in small teams. Connor cares about writing clean code, solving problems thoughtfully, and learning quickly.

He also enjoys learning new things, helping others, science fiction, action and comedy films, and reading educational books.
